 {"id":"bd-10qa5","title":"CAAM: Add [integrations.caam] config section to TOML","description":"Add configuration support for CAAM integration in NTM config file.

## Config Schema

```toml
[integrations.caam]
# Enable CAAM account management
enabled = true

# Path to caam binary (optional, defaults to PATH lookup)
binary_path = ""

# Enable automatic account rotation on rate limit
auto_rotate = true

# Providers to manage (empty = all available)
providers = ["claude", "openai", "gemini"]

# Rate limit detection patterns (in addition to defaults)
rate_limit_patterns = []

# Cooldown before retrying same account (seconds)
account_cooldown = 300

# Alert threshold (percentage of limit)
alert_threshold = 80
```

## Implementation Location
- Update: internal/config/config.go (add CAAMConfig struct)
- Update: internal/config/defaults.go (set defaults)

## Struct Definition

```go
type CAAMConfig struct {
    Enabled           bool     `toml:"enabled"`
    BinaryPath        string   `toml:"binary_path"`
    AutoRotate        bool     `toml:"auto_rotate"`
    Providers         []string `toml:"providers"`
    RateLimitPatterns []string `toml:"rate_limit_patterns"`
    AccountCooldown   int      `toml:"account_cooldown"`
    AlertThreshold    int      `toml:"alert_threshold"`
}
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:49:30.659313954Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T07:09:57.907305322Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T07:09:53.391877677Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-10qa5","depends_on_id":"bd-8o977","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-10ufs","title":"EPIC: Fleet Management Commands (scale, rebalance, review-queue)","description":"## Goal
Provide a cohesive set of commands for managing agent fleet composition, workload distribution, and batch operations.

## Commands in Scope

### ntm scale
Manual scaling to target agent counts without autonomous auto-scaling.
- `ntm scale <session> --cc=N --cod=N --gmi=N`
- Computes delta vs current counts and calls ntm add/kill
- Safe confirmations for scale-down
- Never auto-kills user pane

### ntm rebalance
Manual rebalance suggestions when agents join/leave or workloads become uneven.
- `ntm rebalance <session>` shows imbalance and suggests reassignments
- `ntm rebalance <session> --apply` applies after confirmation
- Detects dead agents and suggests orphan task reassignment

### ntm review-queue
Suggests review prompts for idle agents to keep them productive.
- `ntm review-queue <session>` lists idle agents + recommended prompts
- `ntm review-queue <session> --send` dispatches after confirmation
- Configurable idle threshold and review templates

### ntm send --batch
Explicit batch prompt delivery from files.
- `ntm send <session> --batch file.txt` sends prompts in order
- Supports comments (#) and separators (---) in batch files
- Agent targeting: --filter, --agent, --broadcast

## Design Philosophy
- All commands are **manual** (user-initiated), not autonomous
- All modifications require **confirmation** (unless --force)
- All commands support **--dry-run** for safe preview
- All commands support **--format json** for robot mode
- Graceful degradation when assignment tracking unavailable

## Success Criteria
- Commands work without assignment tracking (basic mode)
- Commands enhance with assignment tracking when available
- Clear, deterministic output for same input state
- No background daemons or automatic triggers
- Comprehensive logging for debugging","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-11mtt","title":"Task: Implement embedded mode catalog with full 80 modes + tiered core subset","description":"# Task: Implement embedded mode catalog with full 80 modes + tiered core subset

## Purpose
Ship the **complete 80-mode taxonomy** from `modes_of_reasoning.md` in `internal/ensemble/modes.go`, with tiered visibility for UX filtering. The catalog must be fully usable without external files.

- **core tier (~28)**: curated default set for UX
- **advanced tier (remaining modes)**: hidden by default but fully accessible via `--all` / `--tier`

## Core Tier (Curated 28)

> These are `tier: core` and must have complete metadata + hand-authored preambles.
> **Mode codes and categories must match the numbering in `modes_of_reasoning.md` (A–L).**
> The groupings below are advisory; final category assignment must follow the doc.

- deductive — Deductive Inference
- mathematical-proof — Mathematical Proof
- formal-verification — Formal Verification
- type-theoretic — Type-Theoretic Reasoning
- inductive — Inductive Generalization
- conceptual-blending — Conceptual Blending
- analogical — Analogical Transfer
- ambiguity-detection — Ambiguity Detection
- edge-case — Edge Case Reasoning
- prototype-reasoning — Prototype Reasoning
- root-cause — Root Cause Analysis
- dependency-mapping — Dependency Mapping
- argument-mapping — Argument Mapping
- adversarial-review — Adversarial Review
- systems-thinking — Systems Thinking
- worst-case — Worst-Case Analysis
- decision-under-uncertainty — Decision Under Uncertainty
- failure-mode — Failure Mode Analysis
- second-order-effects — Second-Order Effects
- resource-allocation — Resource Allocation
- strategic-planning — Strategic Planning
- option-generation — Option Generation
- prioritization — Prioritization
- engineering-design — Engineering Design Reasoning
- test-plan — Test Plan Mode
- compliance — Compliance Lens
- meta-cognitive — Meta-cognitive Monitoring
- perspective-taking — Perspective Taking

## Advanced Tier (Remaining 52)

- All remaining modes from `modes_of_reasoning.md` are included as `tier: advanced`.
- Categories must remain within A–L taxonomy.
- ModeCode **letter must match category** (A→Formal, B→Ampliative, ... L→Meta).

## Implementation Pattern
```go
var EmbeddedModes = []ReasoningMode{
    {
        ID:        "deductive",
        Code:      "A1",
        Tier:      TierCore,
        Name:      "Deductive Inference",
        Category:  CategoryFormal,
        ShortDesc: "Classical logical inference",
        // ...
    },
    // ... all 80 modes
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] **All 80 modes** defined with complete metadata
- [ ] Core tier is exactly the curated 28 list above
- [ ] Advanced modes included and validated (no feature loss)
- [ ] Codes unique and category letters correct
- [ ] Categories limited to A–L taxonomy
- [ ] Modes validated at init time","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:31:09.088788198Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T23:01:02.638087733Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T23:01:02.638023412Z","close_reason":"Implemented full 80-mode embedded catalog in modes.go with 28 core + 52 advanced tier modes. All modes mapped to A-L taxonomy codes. DefaultCatalog() helper and CatalogVersion constant added. 11 comprehensive tests verify: count, tier distribution, validation, uniqueness, category-letter consistency, all categories present, code-to-ID lookup, and ListDefault correctness.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11mtt","depends_on_id":"bd-2qfa5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-11mtt","depends_on_id":"bd-3806s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1ovi8","title":"Task: Implement ensemble loader with user/project override support","description":"Implement user-defined ensemble configuration system with user/project overrides.

## Configuration File Format

### Locations
```
~/.config/ntm/ensembles.yaml
# or project-local
.ntm/ensembles.yaml
```

### Schema
```yaml
ensembles:
  my-security-review:
    display: "Security Review"
    description: "Security-focused code review ensemble"
    modes:
      - id: deductive         # A1
      - id: root-cause        # E2
      - id: worst-case        # G1
      - id: meta-cognitive    # L1
      - id: systems-thinking  # K1
    synthesis:
      strategy: adversarial
      synthesizer_mode: meta-cognitive
    budget:
      per_agent: 5000
      total: 35000
    allow_advanced: true
    agent_distribution:
      claude: 3
      codex: 2
    tags: [security, review]

  extended-architecture:
    extends: architecture-review
    modes:
      - id: formal-verification  # A3
    synthesis:
      strategy: deliberative

aliases:
  sec: my-security-review
  arch: architecture-review
```

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/config.go
type EnsembleConfigFile struct {
    Ensembles map[string]*UserEnsemble `yaml:"ensembles"`
    Aliases   map[string]string        `yaml:"aliases"`
}

type UserEnsemble struct {
    Display           string           `yaml:"display"`
    Description       string           `yaml:"description"`
    Extends           string           `yaml:"extends"`
    Modes             []ModeRef        `yaml:"modes"` // id or code
    Synthesis         SynthesisConfig  `yaml:"synthesis"`
    Budget            BudgetConfig     `yaml:"budget"`
    AllowAdvanced     bool             `yaml:"allow_advanced"`
    AgentDistribution map[string]int   `yaml:"agent_distribution"`
    Tags              []string         `yaml:"tags"`
}

func LoadEnsembleConfig() (*EnsembleConfigFile, error)
func (c *EnsembleConfigFile) GetEnsemble(name string) (*ResolvedEnsemble, error)
func (c *EnsembleConfigFile) ResolveAlias(name string) string
func MergeWithBuiltins(user *EnsembleConfigFile) *EnsembleConfigFile
```

## Validation
- Mode references resolve by id or code
- Strategy values valid (bd-2ztw9)
- Extends references existing ensemble
- No circular extends

## Dependencies
- Built-in ensembles (bd-cph20)
- Mode code support (bd-3806s)
- Strategy definitions (bd-2ztw9)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:41:24.472913894Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T02:57:27.211596389Z","closed_at":"2026-01-24T02:57:27.211557906Z","close_reason":"Complete: ensemble_loader.go with multi-source precedence (embedded < user < project), EnsembleRegistry, GlobalEnsembleRegistry singleton, all tests passing","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ovi8","depends_on_id":"bd-2ztw9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ovi8","depends_on_id":"bd-35e81","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ovi8","depends_on_id":"bd-3806s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ovi8","depends_on_id":"bd-cph20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1pt6v","title":"Implement --pane flag for single-pane diagnosis","description":"Implement --pane flag for single-pane diagnosis.

## What

Add --pane=N flag to diagnose a specific pane instead of all panes.

## Use Cases

- Quick check on suspected problem pane
- Debugging a specific agent
- Reduced output when you know where the issue is

## Implementation

```go
func diagnose(session string, paneFilter *int) DiagnoseResult {
    panes := getAllPanes(session)
    
    if paneFilter != nil {
        // Filter to single pane
        panes = filterToPanes(panes, []int{*paneFilter})
        if len(panes) == 0 {
            return errorResult("PANE_NOT_FOUND", paneFilter)
        }
    }
    
    // Continue with diagnosis on filtered panes
    ...
}
```

## Output Difference

With --pane=4:
```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "filter": {"pane": 4},
  "panes": {
    "healthy": [],
    "rate_limited": [4]
  },
  "recommendations": [{...single recommendation for pane 4...}]
}
```

## Validation

- Error if pane doesn't exist
- Error if pane is control pane (1) unless --allow-control","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:39:20.631366165Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:27:48.615891952Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:27:48.615850244Z","close_reason":"Implemented as part of bd-31e1f diagnose.go - includes --diagnose-pane flag, overall health classification, --diagnose-fix auto-remediation, recommendation generator, and health classification engine","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1pt6v","depends_on_id":"bd-31e1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1v1y","title":"Task: Implement proactive compaction trigger","description":"## Purpose
Automatically trigger context compaction when prediction threshold crossed.

## Trigger Logic
if prediction.ShouldCompact && config.AutoCompact {
    // Determine agent type
    switch pane.Type {
    case AgentClaude:
        send '/compact' to pane
    case AgentCodex:
        send '!compact' to pane  // or equivalent
    case AgentGemini:
        // Gemini-specific compaction
    }
    
    // Wait for compaction to complete
    waitForCompaction(pane)
    
    // Inject recovery context
    injectRecoveryContext(pane)
}

## Scope
- Detect ShouldCompact from predictor
- Send appropriate compact command per agent type
- Wait for compaction completion
- Inject recovery context after compaction
- Notify user of automatic compaction

## Acceptance Criteria
- Compaction triggered before context exhaustion
- Correct command sent per agent type
- Recovery context injected post-compaction
- User notification in dashboard/alerts

## Technical Notes
Reuse session recovery infrastructure

 {"id":"bd-1v922","title":"LimitDetector: Emit events on limit detection","description":"## LimitDetector: Emit Events on Limit Detection

### Purpose
Define the event emission system that notifies subscribers when limits are detected.

### Implementation Details

**File:** Part of `internal/swarm/limit_detector.go`

**Event Channel:**
```go
// NewLimitDetector creates a LimitDetector with event channel.
func NewLimitDetector(cfg *config.SwarmConfig, tracker *ratelimit.RateLimitTracker) *LimitDetector {
    return &LimitDetector{
        Config:    cfg,
        Tracker:   tracker,
        eventChan: make(chan LimitEvent, 100), // Buffered channel
        matcher:   NewPatternMatcher(cfg.LimitPatterns),
    }
}

// Events returns the event channel for consumers.
func (d *LimitDetector) Events() <-chan LimitEvent {
    return d.eventChan
}

// Close cleans up resources.
func (d *LimitDetector) Close() {
    if d.cancel != nil {
        d.cancel()
    }
    close(d.eventChan)
}
```

**Event Consumer Pattern:**
```go
// Example: AutoRespawner consuming events
func (r *AutoRespawner) handleEvents(ctx context.Context) {
    for {
        select {
        case <-ctx.Done():
            return
        case event, ok := <-r.LimitDetector.Events():
            if !ok {
                return // Channel closed
            }
            log.Info("[AutoRespawner] limit_received", 
                "session_pane", event.SessionPane,
                "pattern", event.Pattern)
            go r.handleLimitHit(event)
        }
    }
}
```

**JSON Output (Robot Mode):**
```go
// LimitEventOutput is the JSON format for robot mode.
type LimitEventOutput struct {
    Type        string    `json:"type"`
    SessionPane string    `json:"session_pane"`
    AgentType   string    `json:"agent_type"`
    Pattern     string    `json:"pattern"`
    DetectedAt  string    `json:"detected_at"`
    RawOutput   string    `json:"raw_output,omitempty"`
}
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log event creation: `[LimitDetector] event_created session_pane=%s`
- Log channel send: `[LimitDetector] event_sent session_pane=%s`
- Log channel full warning: `[LimitDetector] event_channel_full dropping=%s`

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Buffered event channel (100 events)
- [ ] Non-blocking send with timeout
- [ ] Channel close on detector shutdown
- [ ] JSON serializable events for robot mode","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:01:08.602967747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:38:23.103730997Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:38:23.103355229Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1v922","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1v922","depends_on_id":"bd-ee3e6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

)

    once.Do(detect)
    return status
}

    path, err := exec.LookPath("caam")
    if err != nil {
        return
    }
    // Run 'caam status --json' and parse

}
```

- Creates new assignment
- Old assignment marked appropriately

### Retry to Specific Pane
- --to-pane targets specific agent
- Validates pane exists
- Validates pane is idle (or --force)

### Batch Retry
- --retry-failed finds all failed
- --agent filter works
- Respects --limit
- Handles partial success

### Strategy Integration
- Uses strategy for agent selection
- --strategy=quality picks best match
- Falls back when no idle agents

### Error Cases
- Retry non-failed bead -> error
- Retry non-existent bead -> error
- No idle agents -> skip or error

## Logging
- Log retry decisions
- Log old vs new assignment
- Log failures

## Test File

 {"id":"bd-2wih","title":"Task: Add --stagger flag to spawn","description":"## Purpose
Add staggering option to spawn command for rate limit avoidance.

## Flag
ntm spawn myproject --cc=5 --stagger=smart|fixed|none

## Stagger Modes
- smart: Use learned optimal delays from RateLimitTracker
- fixed: Use fixed delay (--stagger-delay=15s)
- none: No staggering (default for backward compatibility)

## Implementation
func (s *SpawnCmd) spawnWithStagger(agents []AgentSpec) error {
    delay := s.rateLimitTracker.GetOptimalDelay(agents[0].Provider)
    
    for i, agent := range agents {
        if i > 0 && s.staggerMode \!= 'none' {
            time.Sleep(delay)
        }
        
        if err := s.spawnAgent(agent); err \!= nil {
            if isRateLimitError(err) {
                s.rateLimitTracker.RecordRateLimit(agent.Provider)
                delay = s.rateLimitTracker.GetOptimalDelay(agent.Provider)
                i-- // Retry
                continue
            }
            return err
        }
        s.rateLimitTracker.RecordSuccess(agent.Provider)
    }
    return nil
}

## Acceptance Criteria
- Staggered spawning works
- Retries on rate limit
- Progress shown during spawn
- Default is backward compatible

## Technical Notes
Add flag to existing spawn command","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
